text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, Oliver loved collecting feathers. He'd weave tiny hats from them indoors. One chilly Sunday afternoon, Oliver heard singing. He buzzed over to an old oak tree.

Around its trunk spun a circle of birds. Oliver twittered.

One brown feather drifted downwards.

He snatched it!

Then a loud night song!

More feathers!

Night came.

Orchos owl pellets.

He took pellets home.

Some feathers.

A great feather!

Oliver soared!

No. More feathers.

You find?

A feather owl! A thief!

His talons.

He snatched.

Soft feather.

Why?

A hawk?

Nothing!

Just you!

You?

One.
